headline: Intuitive Machines Wins Missile Tracking Work And Funding As Volatility Persists
summary: Intuitive Machines' subsidiary Lanteris Space Systems was selected by L3Harris Technologies to build 18 spacecraft platforms for the U.S. Space Development Agency's Tranche 3 Tracking Layer missile tracking program. The company also secured a $175 million institutional equity investment to support expansion in space infrastructure and communications technology. For investors watching Intuitive Machines (NasdaqGM:LUNR), these announcements put fresh attention on a stock trading around $18.2,...

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider L3Harris Technologies (LHX) After Its 81% One-Year Surge?
summary: If you are wondering whether L3Harris Technologies' current share price still offers value, it helps to step back and look at how the market has been treating the stock recently and what that might imply for expectations. The shares last closed at US$368.00, with returns of 3.9% over 7 days, 7.3% over 30 days, 20.9% year to date, 81.1% over 1 year, 85.1% over 3 years and 116.8% over 5 years. This gives important context before thinking about what the stock might be worth today. Recent...

headline: L3Harris CFO Change Highlights Missile Solutions IPO And Valuation Story
summary: L3Harris Technologies (NYSE:LHX) has appointed Kenneth Sharp as Chief Financial Officer and Senior Vice President. Former CFO Kenneth Bedingfield will lead the Missile Solutions segment and oversee preparation for a future IPO of that business. The leadership changes align the finance function and missile operations with L3Harris’s long term plans for its portfolio. L3Harris operates in defense electronics, communications systems and mission solutions, where demand is closely linked to long...
